Flutter.tsx — API Reference

Auto-generated from the live Flutter widget catalog. Run bun run docs to regenerate.

Examples

Hello World

The minimal Flutter.tsx app — a MaterialApp with a Scaffold, AppBar, and centered Text. 

import { MaterialApp, Scaffold, AppBar, Center, Text } from 'flutter-tsx';

export const App = () => (
  <MaterialApp title="My App">
    <Scaffold>
      <AppBar title="My App" />
      <Center>
        <Text>Hello, World!</Text>
      </Center>
    </Scaffold>
  </MaterialApp>
);
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: hello-world.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class App extends StatelessWidget {
  const App({super.key});
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(title: 'My App', home: Scaffold(appBar: AppBar(title: Text('My App')), body: Center(child: Text('Hello, World!'))));
  }
}

Counter

Classic stateful counter. useState is rewritten to a StatefulWidget with setState(). 

import { Column, Text, ElevatedButton, useState } from 'flutter-tsx';

export function Counter() {
  const [count, setCount] = useState(0);
  return (
    <Column mainAxisAlignment="center">
      <Text>Count: {count}</Text>
      <ElevatedButton onClick={() => setCount(count + 1)}>
        Increment
      </ElevatedButton>
    </Column>
  );
}
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: counter.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class Counter extends StatefulWidget {
  const Counter({super.key});
  @override
  State<Counter> createState() => _CounterState();
}
class _CounterState extends State<Counter> {
  int count = 0;
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Column(mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center, children: [Text('Count:' + '$count'), ElevatedButton(onPressed: () { setState(() { count = count + 1; }); }, child: Text('Increment'))]);
  }
}

Controlled Text Input

A TextField whose value is kept in useState — the TSX mental model for controlled inputs. 

import { Column, TextField, Text, useState } from 'flutter-tsx';

export function NameEntry() {
  const [name, setName] = useState('');
  return (
    <Column>
      <TextField
        label="Your name"
        onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)}
      />
      <Text>Hello, {name}!</Text>
    </Column>
  );
}
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: text-input.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class NameEntry extends StatefulWidget {
  const NameEntry({super.key});
  @override
  State<NameEntry> createState() => _NameEntryState();
}
class _NameEntryState extends State<NameEntry> {
  String name = '';
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Column(children: [TextField(decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: 'Your name'), onChanged: (value) { setState(() { name = value; }); }), Text('Hello,' + '$name' + '!')]);
  }
}

Light / Dark Toggle

Switch widget controls a boolean state, showing conditional text. 

import { Column, Switch, Text, useState } from 'flutter-tsx';

export function ThemeToggle() {
  const [dark, setDark] = useState(false);
  return (
    <Column mainAxisAlignment="center">
      <Text>{dark ? 'Dark mode' : 'Light mode'}</Text>
      <Switch value={dark} onChange={() => setDark(!dark)} />
    </Column>
  );
}
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: toggle.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class ThemeToggle extends StatefulWidget {
  const ThemeToggle({super.key});
  @override
  State<ThemeToggle> createState() => _ThemeToggleState();
}
class _ThemeToggleState extends State<ThemeToggle> {
  bool dark = false;
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Column(mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center, children: [Text('${dark ? 'Dark mode' : 'Light mode'}'), Switch(value: dark, onChanged: () { setState(() { dark = !dark; }); })]);
  }
}

Tap to Animate

Gesture props + the animate prop. onTap works on any widget (auto-wrapped in a GestureDetector); animate swaps Container for AnimatedContainer so size and color tween on each tap. 

import { Center, Container, Text, useState } from 'flutter-tsx';

export function TapBox() {
  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);
  return (
    <Center>
      <Container
        animate
        duration={300}
        curve="easeInOut"
        width={open ? 240 : 120}
        height={open ? 240 : 120}
        color={open ? 'blue' : 'grey'}
        onTap={() => setOpen(!open)}
      >
        <Center>
          <Text>{open ? 'Tap to shrink' : 'Tap to grow'}</Text>
        </Center>
      </Container>
    </Center>
  );
}
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: tap-to-animate.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class TapBox extends StatefulWidget {
  const TapBox({super.key});
  @override
  State<TapBox> createState() => _TapBoxState();
}
class _TapBoxState extends State<TapBox> {
  bool open = false;
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Center(child: GestureDetector(onTap: () { setState(() { open = !open; }); }, child: AnimatedContainer(duration: Duration(milliseconds: 300), curve: Curves.easeInOut, width: open ? 240 : 120, height: open ? 240 : 120, color: open ? Colors.blue : Colors.grey, child: Center(child: Text('${open ? 'Tap to shrink' : 'Tap to grow'}')))));
  }
}

Profile Card

A Card with nested Column, styled Text, and padding — shows widget composition and TextStyle. 

import { Card, Column, Text, Padding } from 'flutter-tsx';

export const ProfileCard = () => (
  <Card>
    <Padding padding="16">
      <Column>
        <Text style={{ fontSize: 20, fontWeight: 'bold' }}>
          Jane Doe
        </Text>
        <Text style={{ fontSize: 14 }}>Flutter developer</Text>
        <Text style={{ fontSize: 12 }}>jane@example.com</Text>
      </Column>
    </Padding>
  </Card>
);
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: profile-card.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class ProfileCard extends StatelessWidget {
  const ProfileCard({super.key});
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Card(child: Padding(padding: EdgeInsets.all(16), child: Column(children: [Text('Jane Doe', style: TextStyle(fontSize: 20, fontWeight: FontWeight.bold)), Text('Flutter developer', style: TextStyle(fontSize: 14)), Text('jane@example.com', style: TextStyle(fontSize: 12))])));
  }
}

Lifecycle with useEffect

useEffect with a setter call maps to initState — a common one-time setup pattern. 

import { Text, useState, useEffect } from 'flutter-tsx';

export function WelcomeBanner() {
  const [message, setMessage] = useState('Loading...');

  useEffect(() => {
    setMessage('Flutter.tsx ready!');
  }, []);

  return <Text>{message}</Text>;
}
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: lifecycle.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class WelcomeBanner extends StatefulWidget {
  const WelcomeBanner({super.key});
  @override
  State<WelcomeBanner> createState() => _WelcomeBannerState();
}
class _WelcomeBannerState extends State<WelcomeBanner> {
  String message = 'Loading...';
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Text('$message');
  }
  @override
  void initState() {
    super.initState();
    setState(() { message = 'Flutter.tsx ready!'; });
  }
}

Scaffold with FAB

The standard Material shell — AppBar, body, and a FloatingActionButton wired to state. 

import {
  MaterialApp, Scaffold, AppBar, Center, Text,
  FloatingActionButton, useState,
} from 'flutter-tsx';

export function FabDemo() {
  const [count, setCount] = useState(0);
  return (
    <MaterialApp title="FAB Demo">
      <Scaffold>
        <AppBar title="FAB Demo" />
        <Center>
          <Text>Tapped {count} times</Text>
        </Center>
        <FloatingActionButton onClick={() => setCount(count + 1)}>
          <Text>+</Text>
        </FloatingActionButton>
      </Scaffold>
    </MaterialApp>
  );
}
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: fab-scaffold.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class FabDemo extends StatefulWidget {
  const FabDemo({super.key});
  @override
  State<FabDemo> createState() => _FabDemoState();
}
class _FabDemoState extends State<FabDemo> {
  int count = 0;
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(title: 'FAB Demo', home: Scaffold(appBar: AppBar(title: Text('FAB Demo')), floatingActionButton: FloatingActionButton(onPressed: () { setState(() { count = count + 1; }); }, child: Text('+')), body: Center(child: Text('Tapped' + '$count' + 'times'))));
  }
}

Settings Screen

A settings page built from Switch and Slider — demonstrates boolean and numeric state. 

import { Column, Switch, Slider, Text, useState } from 'flutter-tsx';

export function SettingsScreen() {
  const [notifs, setNotifs] = useState(true);
  const [volume, setVolume] = useState(0.8);
  return (
    <Column>
      <Text>Notifications</Text>
      <Switch
        value={notifs}
        onChange={() => setNotifs(!notifs)}
      />
      <Text>Volume</Text>
      <Slider value={volume} onChange={(v) => setVolume(v)} />
    </Column>
  );
}
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: settings.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class SettingsScreen extends StatefulWidget {
  const SettingsScreen({super.key});
  @override
  State<SettingsScreen> createState() => _SettingsScreenState();
}
class _SettingsScreenState extends State<SettingsScreen> {
  bool notifs = true;
  double volume = 0.8;
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Column(children: [Text('Notifications'), Switch(value: notifs, onChanged: () { setState(() { notifs = !notifs; }); }), Text('Volume'), Slider(value: volume, onChanged: (v) { setState(() { volume = v; }); })]);
  }
}

Loading State

useEffect triggers setup; while loading, a CircularProgressIndicator is shown — early return pattern. 

import {
  CircularProgressIndicator, Text, useState, useEffect,
} from 'flutter-tsx';

export function StatusView() {
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
  const [status, setStatus] = useState('');

  useEffect(() => {
    setStatus('Connected');
    setLoading(false);
  }, []);

  if (loading) return <CircularProgressIndicator />;
  return <Text>{status}</Text>;
}
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: loading-state.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class StatusView extends StatefulWidget {
  const StatusView({super.key});
  @override
  State<StatusView> createState() => _StatusViewState();
}
class _StatusViewState extends State<StatusView> {
  bool loading = true;
  String status = '';
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    if (loading) return CircularProgressIndicator();
    return Text('$status');
  }
  @override
  void initState() {
    super.initState();
    setState(() { status = 'Connected'; });
    setState(() { loading = false; });
  }
}

Todo List

A store-backed todo list: add appends, tapping an item removes it. createStore generates a ChangeNotifier; the TextField draft is local useState. 

import {
  Scaffold, AppBar, Column, ListView, ListTile, Checkbox,
  TextField, ElevatedButton, useState, createStore,
} from 'flutter-tsx';

export const useTodos = createStore((set) => ({
  items: [],
  done: [],
  add: (text) => set((s) => ({ items: [...s.items, text] })),
  remove: (text) => set((s) => ({
    items: s.items.where((t) => t != text).toList(),
    done: s.done.where((t) => t != text).toList(),
  })),
  toggle: (text) => set((s) => ({
    done: s.done.contains(text)
      ? s.done.where((t) => t != text).toList()
      : [...s.done, text],
  })),
}));

export function TodoList() {
  const { items, done, add, remove, toggle } = useTodos();
  const [draft, setDraft] = useState('');

  return (
    <Scaffold>
      <AppBar title="Todos" />
      <Column>
        <TextField label="New todo" onChange={(e) => setDraft(e.target.value)} />
        <ElevatedButton onClick={() => add(draft)}>Add</ElevatedButton>
        <ListView>
          {items.map((todo) => (
            <ListTile
              key={todo}
              title={todo}
              leading={<Checkbox value={done.contains(todo)} onChange={(v) => toggle(todo)} />}
              onTap={() => remove(todo)}
            />
          ))}
        </ListView>
      </Column>
    </Scaffold>
  );
}
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: todo-list.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:provider/provider.dart';

class TodosStore extends ChangeNotifier {
  var items = [];
  var done = [];
  void add(dynamic text) {
    items = [...items, text];
    notifyListeners();
  }
  void remove(dynamic text) {
    items = items.where((t) => t != text).toList();
    done = done.where((t) => t != text).toList();
    notifyListeners();
  }
  void toggle(dynamic text) {
    done = done.contains(text)
      ? done.where((t) => t != text).toList()
      : [...done, text];
    notifyListeners();
  }
}

class TodoList extends StatefulWidget {
  const TodoList({super.key});
  @override
  State<TodoList> createState() => _TodoListState();
}
class _TodoListState extends State<TodoList> {
  String draft = '';
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    final todosStore = context.watch<TodosStore>();
    final items = todosStore.items;
    final done = todosStore.done;
    final add = todosStore.add;
    final remove = todosStore.remove;
    final toggle = todosStore.toggle;
    return Scaffold(appBar: AppBar(title: Text('Todos')), body: Column(children: [TextField(decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: 'New todo'), onChanged: (value) { setState(() { draft = value; }); }), ElevatedButton(onPressed: () { add(draft); }, child: Text('Add')), ListView(children: [...items.map((todo) => ListTile(title: Text(todo), leading: Checkbox(value: done.contains(todo), onChanged: (v) { toggle(todo); }), onTap: () { remove(todo); })).toList()])]));
  }
}

Camera

Take a photo with the device camera using useCamera — async handler + plugin method rewrite. 

import { Column, ElevatedButton, Text, useState } from 'flutter-tsx';
import { useCamera } from 'flutter-tsx/plugins';

export const CameraScreen = () => {
  const cam = useCamera();
  const [taken, setTaken] = useState(false);

  const takePhoto = async () => {
    await cam.takePicture();
    setTaken(true);
  };

  return (
    <Column>
      {taken && <Text>Photo saved!</Text>}
      <ElevatedButton onClick={takePhoto}>Take Photo</ElevatedButton>
    </Column>
  );
};
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: camera.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:camera/camera.dart';

class CameraScreen extends StatefulWidget {
  const CameraScreen({super.key});
  @override
  State<CameraScreen> createState() => _CameraScreenState();
}
class _CameraScreenState extends State<CameraScreen> {
  bool taken = false;
  CameraController? _cameraController;
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Column(children: [if (taken) Text('Photo saved!'), ElevatedButton(onPressed: _takePhoto, child: Text('Take Photo'))]);
  }
  @override
  void initState() {
    super.initState();
    availableCameras().then((cameras) {
      if (cameras.isEmpty) return;
      _cameraController = CameraController(cameras.first, ResolutionPreset.medium);
      _cameraController!.initialize().then((_) { if (mounted) setState(() {}); });
    });
  }
  @override
  void dispose() {
    _cameraController?.dispose();
    super.dispose();
  }
  Future<void> _takePhoto() async {
    await _cameraController!.takePicture();
    setState(() { taken = true; });
  }
}

Google Map

Animate the camera to a new location with useMapController — object-arg plugin method rewrite. 

import { Column, ElevatedButton } from 'flutter-tsx';
import { useMapController } from 'flutter-tsx/plugins';

export const MapScreen = () => {
  const map = useMapController();

  const goToParis = async () => {
    await map.animateTo({ lat: 48.8566, lng: 2.3522, zoom: 13 });
  };

  return (
    <Column>
      <ElevatedButton onClick={goToParis}>Fly to Paris</ElevatedButton>
    </Column>
  );
};
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: google-map.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:google_maps_flutter/google_maps_flutter.dart';

class MapScreen extends StatefulWidget {
  const MapScreen({super.key});
  @override
  State<MapScreen> createState() => _MapScreenState();
}
class _MapScreenState extends State<MapScreen> {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Column(children: [ElevatedButton(onPressed: _goToParis, child: Text('Fly to Paris'))]);
  }
  Future<void> _goToParis() async {
    await _mapController?.animateCamera(CameraUpdate.newCameraPosition(CameraPosition(target: LatLng(48.8566, 2.3522), zoom: 13 ?? 12)));
  }
}

Multi-Screen App

A tabbed app composed from separate screen components. <TabView> generates a Scaffold + BottomNavigationBar + IndexedStack (each tab keeps its state). 

import { MaterialApp, TabView, Center, Text } from 'flutter-tsx';

export const HomeScreen = () => (
  <Center><Text>Home</Text></Center>
);

export const ProfileScreen = () => (
  <Center><Text>Profile</Text></Center>
);

export const MultiScreenApp = () => (
  <MaterialApp title="Multi Screen">
    <TabView tabs={[
      { label: 'Home', icon: 'home', screen: <HomeScreen /> },
      { label: 'Profile', icon: 'person', screen: <ProfileScreen /> },
    ]} />
  </MaterialApp>
);
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: multi-screen.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class _FsxTabs0 extends StatefulWidget {
  const _FsxTabs0({super.key});
  @override
  State<_FsxTabs0> createState() => _FsxTabs0State();
}

class _FsxTabs0State extends State<_FsxTabs0> {
  int _index = 0;
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      body: IndexedStack(index: _index, children: [HomeScreen(), ProfileScreen()]),
      bottomNavigationBar: BottomNavigationBar(
        currentIndex: _index,
        onTap: (i) => setState(() => _index = i),
        items: const [BottomNavigationBarItem(icon: Icon(Icons.home), label: 'Home'), BottomNavigationBarItem(icon: Icon(Icons.person), label: 'Profile')],
      ),
    );
  }
}

class HomeScreen extends StatelessWidget {
  const HomeScreen({super.key});
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Center(child: Text('Home'));
  }
}

class ProfileScreen extends StatelessWidget {
  const ProfileScreen({super.key});
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Center(child: Text('Profile'));
  }
}

class MultiScreenApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MultiScreenApp({super.key});
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(title: 'Multi Screen', home: _FsxTabs0());
  }
}

Drawer Menu

A hamburger drawer (the AppBar shows the menu button automatically) whose ListTiles switch the body content. 

import {
  MaterialApp, Scaffold, AppBar, Center, Text,
  Drawer, DrawerHeader, ListView, ListTile, useState,
} from 'flutter-tsx';

const PAGES = ['Home', 'Profile', 'Settings'];

export function DrawerApp() {
  const [page, setPage] = useState(0);
  return (
    <MaterialApp title="Drawer Demo">
      <Scaffold>
        <AppBar title="Drawer Demo" />
        <Drawer>
          <ListView>
            <DrawerHeader><Text>Menu</Text></DrawerHeader>
            <ListTile title="Home" onTap={() => setPage(0)} />
            <ListTile title="Profile" onTap={() => setPage(1)} />
            <ListTile title="Settings" onTap={() => setPage(2)} />
          </ListView>
        </Drawer>
        <Center><Text>{PAGES[page]}</Text></Center>
      </Scaffold>
    </MaterialApp>
  );
}
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: drawer-menu.tsx

import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

final PAGES = ['Home', 'Profile', 'Settings'];

class DrawerApp extends StatefulWidget {
  const DrawerApp({super.key});
  @override
  State<DrawerApp> createState() => _DrawerAppState();
}
class _DrawerAppState extends State<DrawerApp> {
  int page = 0;
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(title: 'Drawer Demo', home: Scaffold(appBar: AppBar(title: Text('Drawer Demo')), drawer: Drawer(child: ListView(children: [DrawerHeader(child: Text('Menu')), ListTile(title: Text('Home'), onTap: () { setState(() { page = 0; }); }), ListTile(title: Text('Profile'), onTap: () { setState(() { page = 1; }); }), ListTile(title: Text('Settings'), onTap: () { setState(() { page = 2; }); })])), body: Center(child: Text('${PAGES[page]}'))));
  }
}

Photo Gallery

Pick photos from the device library with useImagePicker — async plugin method + state counter. 

import { Column, ElevatedButton, Text, useState } from 'flutter-tsx';
import { useImagePicker } from 'flutter-tsx/plugins';

export function PhotoGallery() {
  const picker = useImagePicker();
  const [count, setCount] = useState(0);

  const pick = async () => {
    await picker.pickImage();
    setCount(count + 1);
  };

  return (
    <Column>
      <Text>Photos: {count}</Text>
      <ElevatedButton onClick={pick}>Pick Photo</ElevatedButton>
    </Column>
  );
}
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: photo-gallery.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:image_picker/image_picker.dart';

class PhotoGallery extends StatefulWidget {
  const PhotoGallery({super.key});
  @override
  State<PhotoGallery> createState() => _PhotoGalleryState();
}
class _PhotoGalleryState extends State<PhotoGallery> {
  int count = 0;
  final ImagePicker _imagePicker = ImagePicker();
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Column(children: [Text('Photos:' + '$count'), ElevatedButton(onPressed: _pick, child: Text('Pick Photo'))]);
  }
  Future<void> _pick() async {
    await _imagePicker.pickImage(source: ImageSource.gallery);
    setState(() { count = count + 1; });
  }
}

World Cities

Fly between cities on a map with useMapController — object-arg plugin method with real coordinates. 

import { Column, ElevatedButton, Text } from 'flutter-tsx';
import { useMapController } from 'flutter-tsx/plugins';

export function WorldCities() {
  const map = useMapController();

  const goToParis = async () => {
    await map.animateTo({ lat: 48.8566, lng: 2.3522, zoom: 12 });
  };

  const goToTokyo = async () => {
    await map.animateTo({ lat: 35.6762, lng: 139.6503, zoom: 11 });
  };

  return (
    <Column>
      <Text>World Cities</Text>
      <ElevatedButton onClick={goToParis}>Paris</ElevatedButton>
      <ElevatedButton onClick={goToTokyo}>Tokyo</ElevatedButton>
    </Column>
  );
}
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: world-cities.tsx

import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:google_maps_flutter/google_maps_flutter.dart';

class WorldCities extends StatefulWidget {
  const WorldCities({super.key});
  @override
  State<WorldCities> createState() => _WorldCitiesState();
}
class _WorldCitiesState extends State<WorldCities> {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Column(children: [Text('World Cities'), ElevatedButton(onPressed: _goToParis, child: Text('Paris')), ElevatedButton(onPressed: _goToTokyo, child: Text('Tokyo'))]);
  }
  Future<void> _goToParis() async {
    await _mapController?.animateCamera(CameraUpdate.newCameraPosition(CameraPosition(target: LatLng(48.8566, 2.3522), zoom: 12 ?? 12)));
  }
  Future<void> _goToTokyo() async {
    await _mapController?.animateCamera(CameraUpdate.newCameraPosition(CameraPosition(target: LatLng(35.6762, 139.6503), zoom: 11 ?? 12)));
  }
}

Login Form

A complete login form: two controlled TextFields + a submit handler — onChange, state, and conditionals. 

import {
  Column, TextField, ElevatedButton, Text, useState,
} from 'flutter-tsx';

export function LoginForm() {
  const [email, setEmail] = useState('');
  const [password, setPassword] = useState('');
  const [submitted, setSubmitted] = useState(false);

  const submit = () => {
    setSubmitted(true);
  };

  return (
    <Column>
      <TextField
        label="Email"
        onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}
      />
      <TextField
        label="Password"
        onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
      />
      <ElevatedButton onClick={submit}>Login</ElevatedButton>
      {submitted && <Text>Logged in!</Text>}
    </Column>
  );
}
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: login-form.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class LoginForm extends StatefulWidget {
  const LoginForm({super.key});
  @override
  State<LoginForm> createState() => _LoginFormState();
}
class _LoginFormState extends State<LoginForm> {
  String email = '';
  String password = '';
  bool submitted = false;
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Column(children: [TextField(decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: 'Email'), onChanged: (value) { setState(() { email = value; }); }), TextField(decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: 'Password'), onChanged: (value) { setState(() { password = value; }); }), ElevatedButton(onPressed: _submit, child: Text('Login')), if (submitted) Text('Logged in!')]);
  }
  void _submit() {
    setState(() { submitted = true; });
  }
}

Shared Store

createStore (Zustand-style) generates an idiomatic ChangeNotifier; destructured usage becomes context.watch reads + action calls. 

import { createStore, Column, Text, ElevatedButton } from 'flutter-tsx';

export const useCounter = createStore((set) => ({
  count: 0,
  increment: () => set((s) => ({ count: s.count + 1 })),
}));

export function CounterScreen() {
  const { count, increment } = useCounter();
  return (
    <Column>
      <Text>Count: {count}</Text>
      <ElevatedButton onClick={increment}>Increment</ElevatedButton>
    </Column>
  );
}
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: store.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:provider/provider.dart';

class CounterStore extends ChangeNotifier {
  int count = 0;
  void increment() {
    count = count + 1;
    notifyListeners();
  }
}

class CounterScreen extends StatelessWidget {
  const CounterScreen({super.key});
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    final counterStore = context.watch<CounterStore>();
    final count = counterStore.count;
    final increment = counterStore.increment;
    return Column(children: [Text('Count:' + '$count'), ElevatedButton(onPressed: increment, child: Text('Increment'))]);
  }
}

Async Data + fetch

useAsync runs a future and compiles to a FutureBuilder; fetch() is a built-in HTTP source over the http package. 

import { useAsync, fetch, Center, CircularProgressIndicator, Text } from 'flutter-tsx';

export function Feed() {
  const { data, loading, error } = useAsync(() => fetch('https://api.example.com/posts'));
  if (loading) return <Center><CircularProgressIndicator /></Center>;
  if (error) return <Center><Text>Failed to load</Text></Center>;
  return <Text>{data.body}</Text>;
}
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: async-fetch.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:http/http.dart';
import 'dart:convert';

class _FetchResponse {
  final bool ok;
  final int status;
  final String body;
  const _FetchResponse(this.ok, this.status, this.body);
  dynamic get json => jsonDecode(body);
}

Future<_FetchResponse> _fsxFetch(String url) async {
  final res = await get(Uri.parse(url));
  return _FetchResponse(
    res.statusCode >= 200 && res.statusCode < 300,
    res.statusCode,
    res.body,
  );
}

class Feed extends StatelessWidget {
  const Feed({super.key});
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return FutureBuilder<dynamic>(
      future: _fsxFetch('https://api.example.com/posts'),
      builder: (context, snapshot) {
        if (snapshot.connectionState != ConnectionState.done) {
          return Center(child: CircularProgressIndicator());
        }
        if (snapshot.hasError) {
          return Center(child: Text('Failed to load'));
        }
        final data = snapshot.data!;
        return Text('${data.body}');
      },
    );
  }
}

Bottom Tabs

TabView generates a Scaffold + BottomNavigationBar + IndexedStack (tab state preserved). 

import { TabView, Center, Text } from 'flutter-tsx';

export function Tabs() {
  return (
    <TabView tabs={[
      { label: 'Home', icon: 'home', screen: <Center><Text>Home</Text></Center> },
      { label: 'Profile', icon: 'person', screen: <Center><Text>Profile</Text></Center> },
    ]} />
  );
}

import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class _FsxTabs0 extends StatefulWidget {
  const _FsxTabs0({super.key});
  @override
  State<_FsxTabs0> createState() => _FsxTabs0State();
}

class _FsxTabs0State extends State<_FsxTabs0> {
  int _index = 0;
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      body: IndexedStack(index: _index, children: [Center(child: Text('Home')), Center(child: Text('Profile'))]),
      bottomNavigationBar: BottomNavigationBar(
        currentIndex: _index,
        onTap: (i) => setState(() => _index = i),
        items: const [BottomNavigationBarItem(icon: Icon(Icons.home), label: 'Home'), BottomNavigationBarItem(icon: Icon(Icons.person), label: 'Profile')],
      ),
    );
  }
}

class Tabs extends StatelessWidget {
  const Tabs({super.key});
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return _FsxTabs0();
  }
}

Modals

Imperative showSheet / showDialog map 1:1 to showModalBottomSheet / showDialog. 

import { Column, ElevatedButton, Text, showSheet, showDialog } from 'flutter-tsx';

export function Actions() {
  return (
    <Column>
      <ElevatedButton onClick={() => showSheet(<Text>Cart</Text>)}>Cart</ElevatedButton>
      <ElevatedButton onClick={() => showDialog(<Text>Delete?</Text>)}>Delete</ElevatedButton>
    </Column>
  );
}
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: modals.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class Actions extends StatelessWidget {
  const Actions({super.key});
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Column(children: [ElevatedButton(onPressed: () { showModalBottomSheet(context: context, builder: (context) => Text('Cart')); }, child: Text('Cart')), ElevatedButton(onPressed: () { showDialog(context: context, builder: (context) => Text('Delete?')); }, child: Text('Delete'))]);
  }
}

Navigation

useNavigate rewrites go/push/replace/pop to context.* (go_router). 

import { useNavigate, Column, ElevatedButton, Text } from 'flutter-tsx';

export function Nav() {
  const nav = useNavigate();
  return (
    <Column>
      <Text>Home</Text>
      <ElevatedButton onClick={() => nav.push('/details')}>Open details</ElevatedButton>
    </Column>
  );
}
// GENERATED — do not edit. Source: navigate.tsx
// ignore_for_file: prefer_const_constructors, prefer_const_literals_to_create_immutables, prefer_const_constructors_in_immutables, prefer_const_declarations, file_names, non_constant_identifier_names, constant_identifier_names, use_key_in_widget_constructors, unused_element_parameter, unnecessary_string_interpolations, prefer_adjacent_string_concatenation, unnecessary_to_list_in_spreads, avoid_unnecessary_containers, sized_box_for_whitespace
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:go_router/go_router.dart';

class Nav extends StatefulWidget {
  const Nav({super.key});
  @override
  State<Nav> createState() => _NavState();
}
class _NavState extends State<Nav> {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Column(children: [Text('Home'), ElevatedButton(onPressed: () { context.push('/details'); }, child: Text('Open details'))]);
  }
}

Hooks & Core APIs (12)

Core hooks for state, async data, routing, and i18n, plus composite widgets (TabView) and modals. Native capability hooks — useCamera, useLocation, useNavigate, and more — are in the Native Plugins section below.

useState

Declares a reactive state variable. Rewritten to a Dart StatefulWidget with setState(). 

const [count, setCount] = useState(0);
return <Text>{count}</Text>;
int count = 0;
// setCount(x) -> setState(() { count = x; });
Widget build(BuildContext context) => Text('$count');

useEffect

Runs a side-effect on mount, with optional cleanup. Transpiles to initState + dispose. 

useEffect(() => {
  final id = startTimer();
  return () => cancel(id);
}, []);
@override
void initState() { super.initState(); /* effect */ }
@override
void dispose() { /* cleanup */ super.dispose(); }

createStore

Defines a shared store (Zustand-style). Generates an idiomatic ChangeNotifier, provided at the app root via provider. 

export const useCounter = createStore((set) => ({
  count: 0,
  increment: () => set((s) => ({ count: s.count + 1 })),
}));
class CounterStore extends ChangeNotifier {
  int count = 0;
  void increment() { count = count + 1; notifyListeners(); }
}

useStore

Reads a store in a screen. Destructuring the hook becomes context.watch reads + action calls; the ChangeNotifierProvider is wired into main.dart. 

const { count, increment } = useCounter();
return <Text>{count}</Text>;
final counterStore = context.watch<CounterStore>();
final count = counterStore.count;
final increment = counterStore.increment;

useAsync

Runs a future and exposes { data, loading, error }. Compiles to a FutureBuilder. 

const { data, loading, error } = useAsync(() => fetch(url));
if (loading) return <CircularProgressIndicator />;
return <Text>{data.body}</Text>;
return FutureBuilder(
  future: _fsxFetch(url),
  builder: (context, snapshot) {
    if (snapshot.connectionState != ConnectionState.done) {
      return const CircularProgressIndicator();
    }
    final data = snapshot.data!;
    return Text(data.body);
  },
);

fetch

Built-in HTTP source over the http package. Resolves to a { ok, status, body, json } response; composes with useAsync. 

const { data } = useAsync(() => fetch('https://api.example.com/posts'));
// generated helper over package:http
future: _fsxFetch('https://api.example.com/posts')

useParams

Reads a route path parameter inside a screen (file-based routing). 

const id = useParams('id'); // on /users/[id]
return <Text>{id}</Text>;
final id = GoRouterState.of(context).pathParameters['id']!;

useTranslations

Returns t(key) resolving from locales/*.json; the transpiler generates l10n.dart. 

const t = useTranslations();
return <Text>{t('app.title')}</Text>;
// from generated l10n.dart
Text(t('app.title'))

TabView

Bottom-navigation shell. Generates a Scaffold + BottomNavigationBar + IndexedStack (tab state preserved). 

<TabView tabs={[
  { label: 'Home', icon: 'home', screen: <Home /> },
  { label: 'Profile', icon: 'person', screen: <Profile /> },
]} />
Scaffold(
  body: IndexedStack(index: _index, children: [Home(), Profile()]),
  bottomNavigationBar: BottomNavigationBar(
    currentIndex: _index,
    onTap: (i) => setState(() => _index = i),
    items: const [ /* … */ ],
  ),
)

showSheet / showDialog

Imperative modals. Call from a handler; map 1:1 to showModalBottomSheet / showDialog. 

<ElevatedButton onClick={() => showSheet(<Cart />)}>Cart</ElevatedButton>
showModalBottomSheet(context: context, builder: (context) => Cart())

Gesture props

onTap / onDoubleTap / onLongPress on ANY widget. Native gesture widgets (GestureDetector, InkWell) pass through; everything else is auto-wrapped in a GestureDetector. 

<Container onTap={() => select(id)} onLongPress={() => remove(id)}>
  <Text>{label}</Text>
</Container>
GestureDetector(
  onTap: () { select(id); },
  onLongPress: () { remove(id); },
  child: Container(child: Text(label)),
)

animate

Implicit animation. Add `animate` (+ optional duration/curve) to an animatable widget and its prop changes tween automatically — the transpiler swaps it for its Animated* twin. 

<Container animate duration={300} curve="easeInOut"
  width={open ? 240 : 120}
  color={open ? 'blue' : 'grey'} />
AnimatedContainer(
  duration: Duration(milliseconds: 300),
  curve: Curves.easeInOut,
  width: open ? 240 : 120,
  color: open ? Colors.blue : Colors.grey,
)

Native Plugins (38)

Media (6)

useCameracameraMediaaction hook

Access the device camera to take photos and record video. 

const cam = useCamera();
await cam.initialize();
const photo = await cam.takePicture();
console.log(photo.path);
final cam = CameraController(cameras.first, ResolutionPreset.medium);
await cam.initialize();
final XFile photo = await cam.takePicture();
debugPrint(photo.path);

useImagePickerimage_pickerMediaaction hook

Pick images and videos from the device gallery or camera. 

const picker = useImagePicker();
const image = await picker.pickImage('gallery');
if (image) console.log(image.path);
final file = await ImagePicker().pickImage(source: ImageSource.gallery);

VideoPlayervideo_playerMediawidget

Play videos from network URLs or local files.

<VideoPlayer url="https://example.com/video.mp4" autoplay />
VideoPlayer(_videoController!)

useVideoControllervideo_playerMediaaction hook

Imperatively control a VideoPlayer widget.

const ctrl = useVideoController();
ctrl.play();
ctrl.pause();
_videoController?.play();

useAudioaudioplayersMediaaction hook

Play audio from URLs or local files.

const audio = useAudio();
await audio.play('https://example.com/sound.mp3');
await _audioPlayer.play(UrlSource(url));

CachedNetworkImagecached_network_imageMediawidget

Display and cache network images with placeholder support. 

<CachedNetworkImage url="https://example.com/photo.jpg" width={200} height={200} />
CachedNetworkImage(imageUrl: 'https://example.com/photo.jpg', width: 200, height: 200)

Maps & Location (3)

GoogleMapgoogle_maps_flutterMaps & Locationwidget

Embed an interactive Google Map with markers and camera control. 

<GoogleMap
  initialLat={37.7749}
  initialLng={-122.4194}
  zoom={12}
/>
GoogleMap(
  initialCameraPosition: CameraPosition(
    target: LatLng(37.7749, -122.4194),
    zoom: 12,
  ),
  onMapCreated: (controller) => _mapController = controller,
)

useMapControllergoogle_maps_flutterMaps & Locationaction hook

Imperatively control a GoogleMap widget.

const map = useMapController();
map.animateTo({ lat: 48.8566, lng: 2.3522, zoom: 13 });
_mapController?.animateCamera(CameraUpdate.newCameraPosition(CameraPosition(target: LatLng(48.8566, 2.3522), zoom: 13)))

useLocationgeolocatorMaps & Locationstate hook

Track the device's GPS position in real time.

const { latitude, longitude, accuracy } = useLocation();
return <Text>{latitude}, {longitude}</Text>;
Geolocator.getPositionStream().listen((pos) => setState(() { _lat = pos.latitude; }))

Storage & Data (2)

useStorageshared_preferencesStorage & Dataclient hook

Persist key-value data across app launches (shared preferences). 

const store = useStorage();
await store.set('theme', 'dark');
const theme = await store.get<string>('theme');
final prefs = await SharedPreferences.getInstance();
await prefs.setString('theme', 'dark');
final theme = prefs.getString('theme');

useDatabasesqfliteStorage & Dataclient hook

Query and mutate a local SQLite database.

const db = useDatabase('app.db');
const rows = await db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [1]);
final db = await openDatabase('app.db');
final rows = await db.rawQuery('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [1]);

Security (2)

useSecureStorageflutter_secure_storageSecurityclient hook

Encrypted key-value storage backed by the OS keychain / keystore. 

const vault = useSecureStorage();
await vault.write('token', 'abc123');
const token = await vault.read('token');
const storage = FlutterSecureStorage();
await storage.write(key: 'token', value: 'abc123');
final token = await storage.read(key: 'token');

useBiometricslocal_authSecurityaction hook

Authenticate users with Face ID, fingerprint, or device PIN. 

const bio = useBiometrics();
const ok = await bio.authenticate('Confirm your identity');
if (ok) doSensitiveAction();
final auth = LocalAuthentication();
final ok = await auth.authenticate(localizedReason: 'Confirm your identity');

Files & Assets (4)

pickFilefile_pickerFiles & Assetsfunction

Open the system file picker and return the selected file path. 

const file = await pickFile({ extensions: ['pdf', 'docx'] });
if (file) console.log(file.path, file.name);
final result = await FilePicker.platform.pickFiles(allowedExtensions: ['pdf', 'docx']);
final file = result?.files.single;

loadAssetFiles & Assetsfunction

Load a bundled app asset (text, JSON) by its asset path. 

const json = await loadAsset('assets/config.json');
const config = JSON.parse(json);
final json = await rootBundle.loadString('assets/config.json');

appDirpath_providerFiles & Assetsfunction

Return the path to the app documents directory.

const dir = await appDir();
console.log('Documents:', dir);
final dir = await getApplicationDocumentsDirectory();

tempDirpath_providerFiles & Assetsfunction

Return the path to the temporary directory for scratch files. 

const tmp = await tempDir();
const cachePath = `${tmp}/image.jpg`;
final dir = await getTemporaryDirectory();

Device & System (4)

useDeviceInfodevice_info_plusDevice & Systemstate hook

Read platform, model, OS version, and other device metadata. 

const { platform, model, version, isPhysicalDevice } = useDeviceInfo();
return <Text>{model} running {version}</Text>;
final info = await DeviceInfoPlugin().iosInfo;
final model = info.model;

useConnectivityconnectivity_plusDevice & Systemstate hook

Reactively track network connectivity type (wifi, mobile, none). 

const { isOnline, type } = useConnectivity();
if (!isOnline) return <Text>No connection</Text>;
Connectivity().onConnectivityChanged.listen((result) => setState(() { _type = result; }))

usePermissionpermission_handlerDevice & Systemstate hook

Check and request a runtime permission (camera, microphone, location…). 

const { status, request } = usePermission('camera');
if (status !== 'granted') {
  return <Button onClick={request}>Allow Camera</Button>;
}
final status = await Permission.camera.status;
if (status.isDenied) await Permission.camera.request();

useNotificationsflutter_local_notificationsDevice & Systemaction hook

Schedule and display local push notifications.

const notif = useNotifications();
await notif.show('New message', 'You have 3 unread messages');
await _notifications.show(0, 'New message', 'You have 3 unread messages', null)

Web & Networking (5)

WebViewwebview_flutterWeb & Networkingwidget

Embed a full web browser view inside your app.

<WebView url="https://flutter.dev" />
WebViewWidget(controller: _webViewController)

useWebViewControllerwebview_flutterWeb & Networkingaction hook

Imperatively control a WebView widget.

const wvc = useWebViewController();
wvc.loadUrl('https://flutter.dev');
_webViewController.loadRequest(Uri.parse(url))

launchUrlurl_launcherWeb & Networkingfunction

Open a URL in the system browser or another external app. 

await launchUrl('https://flutter.dev');
await launchUrl('mailto:help@example.com');
await launchUrl(Uri.parse('https://flutter.dev'), mode: LaunchMode.externalApplication)

fetchhttpWeb & Networkingfunction

Make HTTP requests — a familiar fetch-style API over the http package. 

const res = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data');
const data = await res.json();
final res = await http.get(Uri.parse('https://api.example.com/data'));
final data = jsonDecode(res.body);

shareshare_plusWeb & Networkingfunction

Open the OS share sheet to share text, URLs, or files. 

await share('Check out flutter-tsx!', subject: 'Flutter TSX');
await Share.share('Check out flutter-tsx!', subject: 'Flutter TSX')

Auth & Payments (2)

useGoogleSignIngoogle_sign_inAuth & Paymentsaction hook

Sign in users with their Google account.

const auth = useGoogleSignIn();
const user = await auth.signIn();
if (user) console.log(user.email, user.displayName);
final account = await GoogleSignIn().signIn();
print('${account?.email}');

useInAppPurchasein_app_purchaseAuth & Paymentsaction hook

Query products and initiate in-app purchases on iOS and Android. 

const iap = useInAppPurchase();
const products = await iap.getProducts(['com.example.premium']);
await iap.purchase('com.example.premium');
final response = await InAppPurchase.instance.queryProductDetails({'com.example.premium'});
await InAppPurchase.instance.buyNonConsumable(purchaseParam: PurchaseParam(productDetails: response.productDetails.first));

Navigation (2)

Routergo_routerNavigationwidget

Declarative URL-based navigation powered by go_router. 

import { Router, Route } from 'flutter-tsx';

export function App() {
  return (
    <Router>
      <Route path="/" component={HomeScreen} />
      <Route path="/profile/:id" component={ProfileScreen} />
    </Router>
  );
}
MaterialApp.router(
  routerConfig: GoRouter(
    routes: [
      GoRoute(path: '/', builder: (ctx, state) => const HomeScreen()),
      GoRoute(path: '/profile/:id', builder: (ctx, state) => ProfileScreen(id: state.pathParameters['id']!)),
    ],
  ),
)

useNavigatego_routerNavigationaction hook

Imperatively navigate to a route using go_router.

const navigate = useNavigate();
navigate('/profile/42');
navigate(-1); // go back
context.go('/profile/42');
context.pop();

Utility (8)

hapticFeedback.lightUtilityfunction

Trigger a light haptic impact (iOS) / tactile feedback (Android). 

await hapticFeedback.light();
await HapticFeedback.lightImpact();

hapticFeedback.mediumUtilityfunction

Trigger a medium haptic impact.

await hapticFeedback.medium();
await HapticFeedback.mediumImpact();

hapticFeedback.heavyUtilityfunction

Trigger a heavy haptic impact.

await hapticFeedback.heavy();
await HapticFeedback.heavyImpact();

hapticFeedback.vibrateUtilityfunction

Trigger a standard vibration.

await hapticFeedback.vibrate();
await HapticFeedback.vibrate();

clipboard.copyUtilityfunction

Copy text to the clipboard.

await clipboard.copy('Hello, clipboard!');
await Clipboard.setData(const ClipboardData(text: 'Hello, clipboard!'));

clipboard.pasteUtilityfunction

Read the current clipboard contents.

const text = await clipboard.paste();
final data = await Clipboard.getData('text/plain');

systemChrome.setOrientationUtilityfunction

Lock the app to portrait or landscape orientation. 

await systemChrome.setOrientation('portrait');
await SystemChrome.setPreferredOrientations([DeviceOrientation.portraitUp]);

systemChrome.setStatusBarColorUtilityfunction

Set the status bar background color.

await systemChrome.setStatusBarColor('#1a1a2e');
SystemChrome.setSystemUIOverlayStyle(SystemUiOverlayStyle(statusBarColor: Color(0xff1a1a2e)));

Layout (17)

Scaffoldmateriallayout

Implements the basic Material Design visual layout structure.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
appBar unknown PreferredSizeWidget?
body FlutterElement Widget?
floatingActionButton FlutterElement Widget?
floatingActionButtonLocation unknown FloatingActionButtonLocation?
floatingActionButtonAnimator unknown FloatingActionButtonAnimator?
persistentFooterButtons FlutterElement[] List<Widget>?
persistentFooterAlignment string AlignmentDirectional
persistentFooterDecoration unknown BoxDecoration?
drawer FlutterElement Widget?
onDrawerChanged (isOpened: boolean) => void void Function(bool)?
endDrawer FlutterElement Widget?
onEndDrawerChanged (isOpened: boolean) => void void Function(bool)?
bottomNavigationBar FlutterElement Widget?
bottomSheet FlutterElement Widget?
backgroundColor unknown dynamic
resizeToAvoidBottomInset boolean bool?
primary boolean bool
drawerDragStartBehavior string DragStartBehavior
extendBody boolean bool
drawerBarrierDismissible boolean bool
extendBodyBehindAppBar boolean bool
drawerScrimColor unknown dynamic
bottomSheetScrimBuilder (p0: unknown) => FlutterElement Widget? Function(BuildContext, Animation)
drawerEdgeDragWidth number double?
drawerEnableOpenDragGesture boolean bool
endDrawerEnableOpenDragGesture boolean bool
restorationId string String? 
export function Example() {
  return <Scaffold persistentFooterAlignment="example" resizeToAvoidBottomInset={true} primary={true}><Text>Content</Text></Scaffold>;
}
Scaffold(persistentFooterAlignment: 'example', resizeToAvoidBottomInset: true, primary: true, body: Text('Content'))

Paddingmateriallayout

A widget that insets its child by the given padding.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
padding number | [number, number] | [number, number, number, number] EdgeInsetsGeometry ✓
child FlutterElement Widget? 
export function Example() {
  return <Padding><Text>Label</Text></Padding>;
}
Padding(child: Text('Label'))

Alignmateriallayout

A widget that aligns its child within itself and optionally sizes itself based on the child's size.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
alignment string AlignmentGeometry
widthFactor number double?
heightFactor number double?
child FlutterElement Widget? 
export function Example() {
  return <Align alignment="example" widthFactor={16} heightFactor={16}><Text>Label</Text></Align>;
}
Align(alignment: 'example', widthFactor: 16, heightFactor: 16, child: Text('Label'))

Centermateriallayout

A widget that centers its child within itself.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
widthFactor number double?
heightFactor number double?
child FlutterElement Widget? 
export function Example() {
  return <Center widthFactor={16} heightFactor={16}><Text>Label</Text></Center>;
}
Center(widthFactor: 16, heightFactor: 16, child: Text('Label'))

SizedBoxmateriallayout

A box with a specified size.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
width number double?
height number double?
child FlutterElement Widget? 
export function Example() {
  return <SizedBox width={16} height={16}><Text>Label</Text></SizedBox>;
}
SizedBox(width: 16, height: 16, child: Text('Label'))

Stackmateriallayout

A widget that positions its children relative to the edges of its box.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
alignment string AlignmentGeometry
textDirection unknown dynamic
fit 'loose' | 'expand' | 'passthrough' StackFit
clipBehavior unknown dynamic
children FlutterElement[] List<Widget> 
export function Example() {
  return <Stack alignment="example" fit="loose"><Text>Item 1</Text><Text>Item 2</Text></Stack>;
}
Stack(alignment: 'example', fit: StackFit.loose, children: [Text('Item 1'), Text('Item 2')])

Rowmateriallayout

A widget that displays its children in a horizontal array.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
mainAxisAlignment 'start' | 'end' | 'center' | 'spaceBetween' | 'spaceAround' | 'spaceEvenly' MainAxisAlignment
mainAxisSize 'min' | 'max' MainAxisSize
crossAxisAlignment 'start' | 'end' | 'center' | 'stretch' | 'baseline' CrossAxisAlignment
textDirection unknown dynamic
verticalDirection 'up' | 'down' VerticalDirection
textBaseline unknown dynamic
spacing number double
children FlutterElement[] List<Widget> 
export function Example() {
  return <Row mainAxisAlignment="start" mainAxisSize="min" crossAxisAlignment="start"><Text>Item 1</Text><Text>Item 2</Text></Row>;
}
Row(mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.start, mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min, crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start, children: [Text('Item 1'), Text('Item 2')])

Columnmateriallayout

A widget that displays its children in a vertical array.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
mainAxisAlignment 'start' | 'end' | 'center' | 'spaceBetween' | 'spaceAround' | 'spaceEvenly' MainAxisAlignment
mainAxisSize 'min' | 'max' MainAxisSize
crossAxisAlignment 'start' | 'end' | 'center' | 'stretch' | 'baseline' CrossAxisAlignment
textDirection unknown dynamic
verticalDirection 'up' | 'down' VerticalDirection
textBaseline unknown dynamic
spacing number double
children FlutterElement[] List<Widget> 
export function Example() {
  return <Column mainAxisAlignment="start" mainAxisSize="min" crossAxisAlignment="start"><Text>Item 1</Text><Text>Item 2</Text></Column>;
}
Column(mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.start, mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min, crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start, children: [Text('Item 1'), Text('Item 2')])

Flexiblemateriallayout

A widget that controls how a child of a [Row], [Column], or [Flex] flexes.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
flex number int
fit 'tight' | 'loose' FlexFit
child FlutterElement Widget ✓ 
export function Example() {
  return <Flexible flex={8} fit="tight"><Text>Label</Text></Flexible>;
}
Flexible(flex: 8, fit: FlexFit.tight, child: Text('Label'))

Expandedmateriallayout

A widget that expands a child of a [Row], [Column], or [Flex] so that the child fills the available space.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
flex number int
child FlutterElement Widget ✓ 
export function Example() {
  return <Expanded flex={8}><Text>Label</Text></Expanded>;
}
Expanded(flex: 8, child: Text('Label'))

Wrapmateriallayout

A widget that displays its children in multiple horizontal or vertical runs.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
direction 'horizontal' | 'vertical' Axis
alignment 'start' | 'end' | 'center' | 'spaceBetween' | 'spaceAround' | 'spaceEvenly' WrapAlignment
spacing number double
runAlignment 'start' | 'end' | 'center' | 'spaceBetween' | 'spaceAround' | 'spaceEvenly' WrapAlignment
runSpacing number double
crossAxisAlignment 'start' | 'end' | 'center' WrapCrossAlignment
textDirection unknown dynamic
verticalDirection 'up' | 'down' VerticalDirection
clipBehavior unknown dynamic
children FlutterElement[] List<Widget> 
export function Example() {
  return <Wrap direction="horizontal" alignment="start" spacing={16}><Text>Item 1</Text><Text>Item 2</Text></Wrap>;
}
Wrap(direction: Axis.horizontal, alignment: WrapAlignment.start, spacing: 16, children: [Text('Item 1'), Text('Item 2')])

Containermateriallayout

A convenience widget that combines common painting, positioning, and sizing widgets.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
alignment string AlignmentGeometry?
padding number | [number, number] | [number, number, number, number] EdgeInsetsGeometry?
color unknown dynamic
isAntiAlias boolean bool
decoration unknown Decoration?
foregroundDecoration unknown Decoration?
width number double?
height number double?
constraints unknown BoxConstraints?
margin number | [number, number] | [number, number, number, number] EdgeInsetsGeometry?
transform unknown dynamic
transformAlignment string AlignmentGeometry?
child FlutterElement Widget?
clipBehavior unknown dynamic 
export function Example() {
  return <Container alignment="example" isAntiAlias={true} width={16}><Text>Label</Text></Container>;
}
Container(alignment: 'example', isAntiAlias: true, width: 16, child: Text('Label'))

SafeAreamateriallayout

A widget that insets its child with sufficient padding to avoid intrusions by the operating system.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
left boolean bool
top boolean bool
right boolean bool
bottom boolean bool
minimum number | [number, number] | [number, number, number, number] EdgeInsets
maintainBottomViewPadding boolean bool
child FlutterElement Widget ✓ 
export function Example() {
  return <SafeArea left={true} top={true} right={true}><Text>Label</Text></SafeArea>;
}
SafeArea(left: true, top: true, right: true, child: Text('Label'))

CustomScrollViewmateriallayout

A [ScrollView] that creates custom scroll effects using [slivers].

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
scrollDirection 'horizontal' | 'vertical' Axis
reverse boolean bool
controller unknown ScrollController?
primary boolean bool?
physics unknown ScrollPhysics?
scrollBehavior unknown ScrollBehavior?
shrinkWrap boolean bool
center unknown Key?
anchor number double

MergeableMaterialmaterialother

Displays a list of [MergeableMaterialItem] children. The list contains [MaterialSlice] items whose boundaries are either "merged" with adjacent items or separated by a [MaterialGap]. The [children] are distributed along the given [mainAxis] in the same way as the children of a [ListBody]. When the list of children changes, gaps are automatically animated open or closed as needed.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
mainAxis 'horizontal' | 'vertical' Axis
elevation number double
hasDividers boolean bool
children unknown[] List<MergeableMaterialItem>
dividerColor unknown dynamic 
export function Example() {
  return <MergeableMaterial mainAxis="horizontal" elevation={16} hasDividers={true} />;
}
MergeableMaterial(mainAxis: Axis.horizontal, elevation: 16, hasDividers: true)

contextMenuBuilder (editableTextState: unknown) => FlutterElement Widget Function(BuildContext, EditableTextState)?
magnifierConfiguration unknown TextMagnifierConfiguration? 
export function Example() {
  return <SelectableText data="example" textScaleFactor={16} showCursor={true} />;
}

Steppermaterialother

A material stepper widget that displays progress through a sequence of steps. Steppers are particularly useful in the case of forms where one step requires the completion of another one, or where multiple steps need to be completed in order to submit the whole form.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
steps unknown[] List<Step> ✓
controller unknown ScrollController?
physics unknown ScrollPhysics?
type 'vertical' | 'horizontal' StepperType
currentStep number int
onStepTapped (value: number) => void void Function(int)?
onStepContinue unknown dynamic
onStepCancel unknown dynamic
controlsBuilder (details: unknown) => FlutterElement Widget Function(BuildContext, ControlsDetails)?
elevation number double?
margin number | [number, number] | [number, number, number, number] EdgeInsetsGeometry?
connectorColor unknown WidgetStateProperty?
connectorThickness number double?
stepIconBuilder (stepIndex: number, stepState: 'indexed' | 'editing' | 'complete' | 'disabled' | 'error') => FlutterElement Widget? Function(int, StepState)?
stepIconHeight number double?
stepIconWidth number double?
stepIconMargin number | [number, number] | [number, number, number, number] EdgeInsets?
clipBehavior unknown dynamic 
export function Example() {
  return <Stepper type="vertical" currentStep={8} elevation={16} />;
}
Stepper(type: StepperType.vertical, currentStep: 8, elevation: 16)

onEnd unknown dynamic

}

ReorderableListmaterialother

A scrolling container that allows the user to interactively reorder the list items.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
itemBuilder (index: number) => FlutterElement Widget Function(BuildContext, int) ✓
itemCount number int ✓
onReorder (oldIndex: number, newIndex: number) => void void Function(int, int) ✓

onReorderEnd (index: number) => void void Function(int)?

prototypeItem FlutterElement Widget?

reverse boolean bool

shrinkWrap boolean bool
anchor number double
cacheExtent number double?
dragStartBehavior string DragStartBehavior

restorationId string String?
clipBehavior unknown dynamic
autoScrollerVelocityScalar number double?
dragBoundaryProvider () => unknown DragBoundaryDelegate? Function(BuildContext)? 
export function Example() {
  return <ReorderableList itemCount={8} itemExtent={16} scrollDirection="horizontal" />;
}
ReorderableList(itemCount: 8, itemExtent: 16, scrollDirection: Axis.horizontal)

child FlutterElement Widget ✓

enabled boolean bool

child FlutterElement Widget ✓
index number int ✓
enabled boolean bool

Scrollablematerialother

Prop TSX type Dart type Required

controller unknown ScrollController?

clipBehavior unknown dynamic

}
Scrollable(axisDirection: AxisDirection.up, excludeFromSemantics: true, semanticChildCount: 8)

Prop TSX type Dart type Required

verticalDetails unknown ScrollableDetails ✓

excludeFromSemantics boolean bool

dragStartBehavior string DragStartBehavior
hitTestBehavior 'deferToChild' | 'opaque' | 'translucent' HitTestBehavior 
export function Example() {
  return <TwoDimensionalScrollable restorationId="example" excludeFromSemantics={true} diagonalDragBehavior="none" />;
}

indentation unknown TreeSliverIndentationType

addRepaintBoundaries boolean bool
addSemanticIndexes boolean bool
semanticIndexCallback (widget: FlutterElement, localIndex: number) => number int? Function(Widget, int)
semanticIndexOffset number int
findChildIndexCallback (p0: unknown) => number int? Function(Key)? 
export function Example() {
  return <TreeSliver addAutomaticKeepAlives={true} addRepaintBoundaries={true} addSemanticIndexes={true} />;
}
TreeSliver(addAutomaticKeepAlives: true, addRepaintBoundaries: true, addSemanticIndexes: true)

child FlutterElement Widget ✓

}

TapRegionmaterialother

A widget that defines a region that can detect taps inside or outside of itself and any group of regions it belongs to, without participating in the [gesture disambiguation](https://flutter.dev/to/gesture-disambiguation) system (other than to consume tap down events if [consumeOutsideTaps] is true).

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
child FlutterElement Widget? ✓
enabled boolean bool
behavior 'deferToChild' | 'opaque' | 'translucent' HitTestBehavior
onTapOutside (event: unknown) => void void Function(PointerDownEvent)?
onTapInside (event: unknown) => void void Function(PointerDownEvent)?
onTapUpOutside (event: unknown) => void void Function(PointerUpEvent)?
onTapUpInside (event: unknown) => void void Function(PointerUpEvent)?
groupId unknown Object?
consumeOutsideTaps boolean bool
debugLabel string String? 
export function Example() {
  return <TapRegion enabled={true} behavior="deferToChild" consumeOutsideTaps={true}><Text>Label</Text></TapRegion>;
}
TapRegion(enabled: true, behavior: HitTestBehavior.deferToChild, consumeOutsideTaps: true, child: Text('Label'))

child FlutterElement Widget? ✓

onTapOutside (event: unknown) => void void Function(PointerDownEvent)?
onTapInside (event: unknown) => void void Function(PointerDownEvent)?

onTapUpInside (event: unknown) => void void Function(PointerUpEvent)?

debugLabel string String?
groupId unknown Object?

}

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
groupId unknown Object

padding number | [number, number] | [number, number, number, number] EdgeInsetsGeometry

suffixMode 'never' | 'editing' | 'notEditing' | 'always' OverlayVisibilityMode

clearButtonSemanticLabel string String?
keyboardType 'text' | 'number' | 'phone' | 'email' | 'url' | 'multiline' TextInputType?
textInputAction 'none' | 'unspecified' | 'done' | 'go' | 'search' | 'send' | 'next' | 'previous' | 'continueAction' | 'join' | 'route' | 'emergencyCall' | 'newline' TextInputAction?
textCapitalization 'words' | 'sentences' | 'characters' | 'none' TextCapitalization

strutStyle unknown StrutStyle?

textAlignVertical unknown TextAlignVertical?

toolbarOptions unknown ToolbarOptions?

obscuringCharacter string String

autocorrect boolean bool?
smartDashesType 'disabled' | 'enabled' SmartDashesType?
smartQuotesType 'disabled' | 'enabled' SmartQuotesType?
enableSuggestions boolean bool
maxLines number int?
minLines number int?
expands boolean bool
maxLength number int?

onEditingComplete unknown dynamic

onTapOutside (event: unknown) => void void Function(PointerDownEvent)?
onTapUpOutside (event: unknown) => void void Function(PointerDownEvent)?

cursorWidth number double
cursorHeight number double?
cursorRadius unknown dynamic
cursorOpacityAnimates boolean bool
cursorColor unknown dynamic
selectionHeightStyle unknown dynamic
selectionWidthStyle unknown dynamic
keyboardAppearance unknown dynamic
scrollPadding number | [number, number] | [number, number, number, number] EdgeInsets
dragStartBehavior string DragStartBehavior
enableInteractiveSelection boolean bool?
selectAllOnFocus boolean bool?
selectionControls unknown TextSelectionControls?

scrollController unknown ScrollController?
scrollPhysics unknown ScrollPhysics?
autofillHints string[] List<String>?
contentInsertionConfiguration unknown ContentInsertionConfiguration?
clipBehavior unknown dynamic
restorationId string String?
scribbleEnabled boolean bool
stylusHandwritingEnabled boolean bool
enableIMEPersonalizedLearning boolean bool
contextMenuBuilder (editableTextState: unknown) => FlutterElement Widget Function(BuildContext, EditableTextState)?
spellCheckConfiguration unknown SpellCheckConfiguration?
magnifierConfiguration unknown TextMagnifierConfiguration? 
export function Example() {
  return <CupertinoTextField placeholder="example" prefixMode="never" suffixMode="never" />;
}
CupertinoTextField(placeholder: 'example', prefixMode: OverlayVisibilityMode.never, suffixMode: OverlayVisibilityMode.never)

CupertinoTextFormFieldRowcupertinoother

Creates a [CupertinoFormRow] containing a [FormField] that wraps a [CupertinoTextField].

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
prefix FlutterElement Widget?
padding number | [number, number] | [number, number, number, number] EdgeInsetsGeometry?
controller unknown TextEditingController?
initialValue string String?
focusNode unknown FocusNode?
decoration unknown BoxDecoration?
keyboardType 'text' | 'number' | 'phone' | 'email' | 'url' | 'multiline' TextInputType?
textCapitalization 'words' | 'sentences' | 'characters' | 'none' TextCapitalization
textInputAction 'none' | 'unspecified' | 'done' | 'go' | 'search' | 'send' | 'next' | 'previous' | 'continueAction' | 'join' | 'route' | 'emergencyCall' | 'newline' TextInputAction?
style TextStyleProps TextStyle?
strutStyle unknown StrutStyle?
textDirection unknown dynamic
textAlign unknown dynamic
textAlignVertical unknown TextAlignVertical?
autofocus boolean bool
readOnly boolean bool
toolbarOptions unknown ToolbarOptions?
showCursor boolean bool?
obscuringCharacter string String
obscureText boolean bool
autocorrect boolean bool
smartDashesType 'disabled' | 'enabled' SmartDashesType?
smartQuotesType 'disabled' | 'enabled' SmartQuotesType?
enableSuggestions boolean bool
maxLines number int?
minLines number int?
expands boolean bool
maxLength number int?
onChange (value: string) => void void Function(String)?
onTap () => void void Function()?
onEditingComplete unknown dynamic
onFieldSubmitted (value: string) => void void Function(String)?
onSaved (newValue: string) => void void Function(String?)?
validator (value: string) => string String? Function(String?)?
inputFormatters unknown[] List<TextInputFormatter>?
enabled boolean bool?
cursorWidth number double
cursorHeight number double?
cursorColor unknown dynamic
keyboardAppearance unknown dynamic
scrollPadding number | [number, number] | [number, number, number, number] EdgeInsets
enableInteractiveSelection boolean bool
selectionControls unknown TextSelectionControls?
scrollPhysics unknown ScrollPhysics?
autofillHints string[] List<String>?
autovalidateMode 'disabled' | 'always' | 'onUserInteraction' | 'onUnfocus' | 'onUserInteractionIfError' AutovalidateMode
placeholder string String?
placeholderStyle TextStyleProps TextStyle?
contextMenuBuilder (editableTextState: unknown) => FlutterElement Widget Function(BuildContext, EditableTextState)?
spellCheckConfiguration unknown SpellCheckConfiguration?
selectionHeightStyle unknown dynamic
selectionWidthStyle unknown dynamic
restorationId string String? 
export function Example() {
  return <CupertinoTextFormFieldRow initialValue="example" textCapitalization="words" textInputAction="none" />;
}
CupertinoTextFormFieldRow(initialValue: 'example', textCapitalization: TextCapitalization.words, textInputAction: TextInputAction.none)

CupertinoTextSelectionToolbarcupertinoother

An iOS-style text selection toolbar.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
anchorAbove unknown dynamic ✓
anchorBelow unknown dynamic ✓
children FlutterElement[] List<Widget> ✓
toolbarBuilder (anchorAbove: unknown, anchorBelow: unknown, child: FlutterElement) => FlutterElement Widget Function(BuildContext, dynamic, dynamic, Widget) 
export function Example() {
  return <CupertinoTextSelectionToolbar><Text>Item 1</Text><Text>Item 2</Text></CupertinoTextSelectionToolbar>;
}
CupertinoTextSelectionToolbar(children: [Text('Item 1'), Text('Item 2')])

CupertinoTextSelectionToolbarButtoncupertinoother

A button in the style of the iOS text selection toolbar buttons.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
onClick unknown dynamic
child FlutterElement Widget ✓ 
export function Example() {
  return <CupertinoTextSelectionToolbarButton><Text>Label</Text></CupertinoTextSelectionToolbarButton>;
}
CupertinoTextSelectionToolbarButton(child: Text('Label'))

CupertinoThemecupertinoother

Applies a visual styling theme to descendant Cupertino widgets.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
data unknown CupertinoThemeData ✓
child FlutterElement Widget ✓ 
export function Example() {
  return <CupertinoTheme><Text>Label</Text></CupertinoTheme>;
}
CupertinoTheme(child: Text('Label'))

InheritedCupertinoThemecupertinoother

Provides a [CupertinoTheme] to all descendants.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
theme unknown CupertinoTheme ✓
child FlutterElement Widget ✓ 
export function Example() {
  return <InheritedCupertinoTheme><Text>Label</Text></InheritedCupertinoTheme>;
}
InheritedCupertinoTheme(child: Text('Label'))

ActionIconThemeDatamaterialtype

A [ActionIconThemeData] that overrides the default icons of [BackButton], [CloseButton], [DrawerButton], and [EndDrawerButton] with [ActionIconTheme.of] or the overall [Theme]'s [ThemeData.actionIconTheme].

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
backButtonIconBuilder () => FlutterElement Widget Function(BuildContext)?
closeButtonIconBuilder () => FlutterElement Widget Function(BuildContext)?
drawerButtonIconBuilder () => FlutterElement Widget Function(BuildContext)?
endDrawerButtonIconBuilder () => FlutterElement Widget Function(BuildContext)?

Describes how [Scrollable] widgets behave for [MaterialApp]s.

MaterialPointArcTweenmaterialtype

A [Tween] that interpolates an [Offset] along a circular arc.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
begin unknown dynamic
end unknown dynamic

MaterialRectArcTweenmaterialtype

A [Tween] that interpolates a [Rect] by having its opposite corners follow circular arcs.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
begin unknown dynamic
end unknown dynamic

MaterialRectCenterArcTweenmaterialtype

A [Tween] that interpolates a [Rect] by moving it along a circular arc from [begin]'s [Rect.center] to [end]'s [Rect.center] while interpolating the rectangle's width and height.

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
begin unknown dynamic
end unknown dynamic

CupertinoBasedMaterialThemeDatamaterialtype

A class for creating a Material theme with a color scheme based off of the colors from a [CupertinoThemeData]. This is intended to be used only in the case when a Material widget is unable to find a Material theme in the tree, but is able to find a Cupertino theme. Most often this will occur when a Material widget is used inside of a [CupertinoApp].

Prop TSX type Dart type Required
themeData unknown CupertinoThemeData ✓

CallbackActionmaterialtype
